title: Interlocking, reconfigurable, reconstitutable, reformable cell-based space system
abstract: Cell-based systems may interlock in a reconfigurable configuration to support a mission. Space systems, for example, of a relatively large size may be assembled using an ensemble of individual âcellsâ, which are individual space vehicles. The cells may be held together via magnets, electromagnets, mechanical interlocks, etc. The topology or shape of the joined cells may be altered by cells hopping, rotating, or ârollingâ along the joint ensemble. The cells may be multifunctional, mass producible units. Rotation of cell faces, or of components within cells, may change the functionality of the cell. The cell maybe collapsible for stowage or during launch.
claims: 1. A cell, comprising: a frame comprising a plurality of rails, a plurality of motion-control guides, or both; a plurality of tractors, each tractor of the plurality of tractors configured to move along a respective rail or motion-control guide of the plurality of rails and/or the plurality of motion-control guides; and a component integrated with or located on at least one of the plurality of tractors, the component comprising a payload, wherein the plurality of tractors are configured to facilitate a respective connection of the cell with at least one other cell, the cell is a space vehicle, and the payload comprises an element that faces externally away from the frame and is configured to capture images, transmit data, receive data, detect radiation, detect temperature, detect light, sense external phenomena, transmit power to an entity external to the cell, generate external electromagnetic fields, or any combination thereof.
